title: Iran has always been the ‘major threat’ against the US
description: Former Navy SEAL and FBI special agent Jonathan Gilliam says Iran has always been the “major threat” against the US.  Mr Gilliam told Sky News Australia that Iran has killed Americans around the world.  “They’ve killed more Americans and citizens around the world than any other nation since Vietnam.”

title: ‘Evil alliance’: Iran’s connection with China and Russia should be a ‘concern’ for the US
description: Former Navy SEAL and FBI special agent Jonathan Gilliam says there are questions about whether China and Russia are giving Iran “assets” to attack the US.  Mr Gilliam told Sky News Australia that it would be a “concern” if China and Russia were involved.  “I think I could see happening because China, Russia, and Iran are so closely related and work together in this evil alliance.”

title: ‘Uncomfortable truth’: Labor exposed as energy-rich nation ‘relies on imports’
description: Sky News host Jaimee Rogers weighs in on “one of the largest energy shocks” in modern history as the fuel crisis deepens.   “But here is the uncomfortable truth,” Ms Rogers said.  “Australia is not in a strong position to absorb a shock like this. We rely on imports for around 90 per cent of our fuel.  “We hold between 30 and 40 days of supply — well below the 90-day minimum recommended by the International Energy Agency.”

title: ‘Our own making’: Iran war exposes Australia’s sovereign energy vulnerabilities
description: Shadow Small Business Minister Jacinta Nampijinpa Price calls out the “dangerous green ideology” which the Labor government is pushing.  “It’s one of our own making, in many ways,” Ms Price told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers.   “Oil refineries have been closed under both Labor and Coalition governments in the past.  “We also need to … start relying on our own sovereign energy sources.”

title: Government faces backlash over visa for preacher promoting anti-Jewish rhetoric
description: Sky News host James Macpherson details how the Labor government has granted a visa to an Islamic hate preacher.  “How is it that the Albanese government can insist it has no tolerance for Muslim hate preachers whilst, at the exact same time, issuing visas to Muslim hate preachers,” Mr Macpherson said.   “This morning it was revealed that a Muslim preacher who has described Jews as 'despicable', and blamed them for everything short of Melbourne's weather, is right now in Australia, having been granted a visa to speak at Muslim conferences in Melbourne and Sydney.  “If they were serious about social cohesion, they wouldn't be issuing Jew hating Islamic preachers with visitor visas.”

title: ‘Iran is not going to back down in 48 hours’: Trump’s stark warning to Iran analysed
description: Sky News Defence Analyst Peter Jennings weighs in on US President Donald Trump's recent threats towards Iran.   “Iran is not going to back down in 48 hours,” Mr Jennings told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers.   “The United States is going to continue its bombing campaign.”

title: ‘Watching carefully’: China helping Iran ‘very considerably’ as tensions in the region rise
description: Sky News Defence Analyst Peter Jennings details how China and Russia thrive amid the Iran conflict.  “China in particular is very happy to see America confronted in the Middle East,” Mr Jennings told Sky News host Jaimee Rogers.   “China is watching this pretty carefully … China is also helping Iran very considerably.  “China is being a wilful helper of Iran here, and I am frankly that is something not being called out more by Americans and others.”

title: Relative of Iranian terror mastermind taken into custody by ICE in US
description: Sky News host James Macpherson discusses ICE arresting relatives of the slain Iranian terror mastermind General Qasem Soleimani.  “US immigration officials are doing a much better job than ours. They've just arrested the grandniece of former Iranian terror mastermind General Qasem Soleimani,” Mr Macpherson said.   “She should consider herself fortunate that US immigration officials got to her before Iran's morality police.  “ICE took an interest after her mother - also enjoying the spoils of American life thanks to the Biden Administration - took to social media to cheer attacks on US soldiers.”

title: Australians receive ‘mixed messages’ from Labor government amid fuel crisis
description: Sky News host James Macpherson details how Australians are continuingly receiving mixed messages from the Labor government over the ongoing fuel crisis.   “We're continuing to get mixed messages from the government when it comes to fuel supply,” Mr Macpherson said.  “On the one hand, the government insists there is no crisis. None at all.  “On the other hand, our energy minister has taken to social media to offer mechanical tips.”

title: ‘Out of control’: UK faces ‘staggering’ rise in non-English speaking students at schools
description: Spike Online Editor Tom Slater discusses the rise in non-English speaking students across the United Kingdom.   “The numbers here are frankly staggering,” Mr Slater told Sky News host James Macpherson.   “It points to the fact that both the pace of immigration and the scale of the integration challenge that it poses are enormous.  “I don’t think many people in the UK would even be aware that this has gotten so out of control.”

title: German men now required to seek military approval to leave country for over three months
description: Spike Online Editor Tom Slater discusses how German men must ask the military for permission to leave the country for more than three months.   “The fact that even the German political class are making these kinds of moves, I think, shows that the penny has dropped with leaders across Europe,” Mr Slater told Sky News host James Macpherson.   “About the new threats that confront us.”

title: 'Just one issue': NYT under fire after NATO naming blunder in Trump piece
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond discusses the New York Times botching the name of NATO in a headline while covering Donald Trump’s comments on the alliance.  “I mean, how could you possibly have a North American Treaty Organisation without America?” Mr Bond said.   “Just one issue. It's not the North American Treaty Organisation.  “I'm not sure how you can misstate the full name of NATO in a newspaper of record.”

title: ‘The couple need money’: Royal ‘gossip’ key to Harry and Meghan’s Netflix lifeline
description: TalkTV host Mark Dolan discusses Prince Harry and Meghan Markle being offered a lifeline from Netflix to work on a new project.   “The couple need money, they do have a couple of million dollars in the bank, but they are getting through the cash very quickly,” Mr Dolan told Sky News host Caleb Bond.   “The only thing Netflix want and the only thing any potential publisher wants is royal gossip.”

title: ‘Open the F****n Strait’: Trump doubles down on ultimatum to Iran in fresh threat
description: US President Donald Trump has reiterated his warning to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in a social media post.

title: Iranian state media responds to US pilot rescue with contested shootdown claims
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claims it shot down several US aircraft involved in the search for the missing American officer.  Iranian state media released a statement saying, “'By God's grace, the American enemy aircraft that was searching for the downed fighter pilot was destroyed at the hands of the soldiers of Islam.”  Iran's claims, which are yet to be independently verified, have been disputed by the US.
